pattern: A traversal from the root over the tree, refusing to enter blocked nodes, counts exactly the component still reachable.
hints: Build an adjacency list but treat restricted nodes as if they were removed.
Start a depth-first or breadth-first search from node 0, never stepping onto a restricted node.
The answer is simply how many nodes your search visits before it is forced to stop.

edge_inputs.description: two nodes, the only neighbour is restricted
a star where most leaves are blocked
a path cut in the middle
blocking a hub isolates an entire subtree
restricted node not on any reachable path is irrelevant

stress.description: large random tree with a random restricted subset, root always free
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randint(0, v - 1)
        edges.append([p, v])
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    k = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    restricted = rng.sample(range(1, n), k)
    return [n, edges, restricted]
